items.hook: Seinfeld’s Jason Alexander apologizes to Courtney Stodden for ‘inappropriate’ sketch made when she was a teenager
items.body:
The actor, who is one of the writers credited for the Funny or Die sketch, said he was ‘deeply sorry for any harm or distress’ caused by the 2012 skit

items.hook: In the United States, William Austin Burt patents the typographer, a precursor to the typewriter.
items.body: William Austin Burt was an American inventor, legislator, surveyor, and millwright. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Leviticus review – queer desire is a deadly curse in haunting horror
items.body:
Sundance film festival: Conversion therapy has gory results in a smart and surprisingly romantic debut feature from Australian writer-director Adrian Chiarella

Something rather nasty is unfolding in Sundance horror

items.hook: Smoky is a 1946 American Western film directed by Louis King and starring Fred MacMurray, Anne Baxter and Bruce Cabot.
items.body: The film was produced and distributed by 20th Century Fox. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Children’s literature festival criticised after requests that comments on diversity be deleted
items.body: John McLay, one of the founders of the festival in Bath, contacted writers who had noted a lack of diversity in the programme to request they remove comments posted to social media

items.hook: A grief therapist told me I should live to honor my son. How would I do that?
items.body:
After my 22-month-old child died, music and poetry brought catharsis and comfort

The day Renzo had his aneurysm, in the afternoon before it happened, I distinctly remember telling him that it was going to be a pleasure to watch him

items.hook: Comet Hale–Bopp is discovered; it becomes visible to the naked eye on Earth nearly a year later.
items.body: Comet Hale–Bopp is a long-period comet that was one of the most widely observed of the 20th century and one of the brightest seen for many decades. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Begin of the Hyde Park Riots.
items.body: The Reform League was established in 1865 to press for manhood suffrage and the ballot in Great Britain. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Servants of the Empire is a young reader science fiction novel series by Jason Fry.
items.body: Set in the Star Wars universe, the novels are affiliated with the television series Star Wars Rebels and follow the Rebels character Zare Leonis. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The end of Heartstopper is a bittersweet loss – it’s never felt scarier to be young and queer | Alim Kheraj
items.body:
The gay Netflix romance maintained a political edge despite rollbacks to queer and trans rights

items.hook: The International Agreement on the Neutrality of Laos is signed.
items.body: The International Agreement on the Neutrality of Laos was an international agreement signed in Geneva on July 23, 1962, between 14 states, including Laos, as a result of the International Conference on the Settlement of the Laotian Question, which lasted from May 16, 1961, to July 23, 1962. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Pompeii: Out of Time With Tom Hiddleston review – you will cringe so hard you turn to cheese
items.body:
The actor emotes his way through Vesuvius’s catastrophic eruption in a docudrama that is embarrassing and exhausting – but undeniably entertaining. Prepare to be Hiddlestoned!

There’s such a fine line between “cheese” and “cringe”,

items.hook: The Serpentine Assassin is the second of the short series of fast-paced, action-based adventure of Agent 13: The Midnight Avenger, written by Flint Dille and David Marconi in a style reminiscent of popular 1930s pulps.
items.body: It picked up directly from the story where the previous book, The Invisible Empire, ended. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Dragon in the Sea (1956), also known as Under Pressure from its serialization, is a novel by Frank Herbert.
items.body: It was first serialized in Astounding magazine from 1955 to 1956, then reworked and published as a standalone novel in 1956. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The City We Became is a 2020 urban fantasy novel by American writer N.
items.body: It was developed from her short story "The City Born Great,” first published in her collection How Long 'til Black Future Month? One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Saxophonist Plas Johnson, known for solo on The Pink Panther Theme, dies aged 94
items.body:
Jazz saxophonist – who recorded with artists Frank Sinatra, BB King and more – died in Los Angeles, reports say

items.hook: Phạm Tuân becomes the first Vietnamese citizen and the first Asian in space when he flies aboard the Soyuz 37 mission as an Intercosmos Research Cosmonaut.
items.body: Phạm Tuân is a retired Vietnamese Air Force fighter pilot and cosmonaut. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The election of Pope John V following the death of Pope Benedict II two months prior.
items.body: Pope John V was the bishop of Rome from 23 July 685 to his death on 2 August 686. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
